title: cattleya coccinea subsp. pygmaea
intro: Cattleya coccinea subsp. pygmaea is a rare and miniature species of orchid, characterized by its small size and vibrant red flowers. In traditional medicine, this plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cattleya coccinea subsp. pygmaea are said to include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the roots in water or using them as a tincture, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
